response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map work, plus solid distributed/high-throughput systems exposure at Sociomantic. However, the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is barely met: only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, no Rust, and the dominant languages across recent roles are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Could be worth a screening call given the geospatial fit and demonstrated ability to work across stacks, but expect a steep ramp on the primary language.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Green Map)
Background in low-latency, high-throughput services with async I/O at Sociomantic
Demonstrated polyglot adaptability and willingness to learn new stacks
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and over 10 years old; no Rust experience
No direct navigation/routing systems or map data pipeline background
Recent work centers on web/TypeScript stacks, far from systems-level tile generation

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent modern C++ / Rust experience
